entries.value: E-commerce conversion rate target is 2-4% for most verticals. Fashion/apparel averages 1.5-2.5%. Electronics 1-2%. Health/beauty 3-4%. Track by traffic source (paid vs organic), device (mobile vs desktop), and landing page. Mobile conversion is typically 40-60% of desktop.

Cart abandonment rate averages 70% industry-wide. Below 60% is excellent. Top reasons: unexpected shipping costs (48%), required account creation (24%), complex checkout (18%). Implement abandoned cart emails within 1 hour for best recovery (10-15% recovery rate).

Overall return rate should stay below 10%. Fashion/apparel averages 20-30% (size issues). Electronics 5-8%. Free returns increase orders but watch net revenue. Track by reason code: wrong-size (fixable with size guides), defective (quality issue), not-as-described (content issue).

Average order value (AOV) is total revenue / number of orders. Increase AOV with: bundle pricing, free shipping thresholds (set 20% above current AOV), cross-sell recommendations, tiered discounts (spend $100 save 10%, spend $150 save 15%). A 10% AOV increase has the same revenue impact as 10% more traffic.

Customer lifetime value (CLV) should be at least 3x customer acquisition cost (CAC). CLV = average order value x purchase frequency x customer lifespan. Track CLV by acquisition channel. Email and referral customers typically have 2-3x higher CLV than paid acquisition.

Inventory turnover (COGS / average inventory) target is 4-6x per year for apparel. Below 4x means excess inventory tying up cash. Above 8x may indicate stockout risk. Track by category and season. Set reorder points at 2-3 weeks of supply for fast movers.

Standard shipping should arrive within 5-7 business days. Express within 2-3 days. Free shipping threshold should be set 20-30% above current AOV to encourage larger orders. Same-day/next-day shipping is expected by 30% of online shoppers.

Low stock alert when inventory drops below 2 weeks of average daily sales. Out-of-stock alert at 3 days of supply. Dead stock threshold: no sales in 90 days. Overstock threshold: more than 6 months of supply at current sell-through rate.

Use psychological pricing (e.g., $49.99 vs $50). Implement dynamic pricing for competitive categories. Never discount more than 30% off full price (destroys brand value). Use limited-time offers with countdown timers. A/B test price points on 10% of traffic before rolling out.

Segment customers using RFM analysis: Recency (days since last order), Frequency (orders in 12 months), Monetary (total spend). Champions (high R+F+M): reward with early access. At-risk (low R, high F+M): win-back campaigns. New customers: nurture sequence within 7 days of first order.
